evidence_excerpt: Severe jaundice persisted with progressive liver atrophy and ascites; later complicated by CMV pneumonia requiring ICU and ventilator support, and he died of respiratory failure. Autopsy confirmed hepatic SOS.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent severe jaundice with progressive liver atrophy and increased ascites; abdominal distention and edema.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: R-CHOP reduced his body temperature and serum LDH concentration.
After the second cycle of R-CODOX-M/IVAC, AST/ALT rose to 516/288 IU/L and bilirubin to 5.5 mg/dL with jaundice, edema, weight gain, and ascites.
Initiated nafamostat mesilate, glycyrrhizic acid, and ursodeoxycholic acid for severe cholestatic-type liver injury.
Severe jaundice persisted; liver function did not recover. CT showed progressive liver atrophy and increased ascites.
Developed CMV pneumonia; admitted to ICU and received ventilator support.
Pneumonia worsened and he died of respiratory failure.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact dates are not provided; timeline inferred from the clinical course figure (2011/9–2012/3). Hepatic SOS was suspected clinically but confirmed at autopsy; index time anchored to the documented lymphoma diagnosis and treatment initiation.
